summary: The Docker-based reproduction was prepared but not fully executed due to build environment constraints. The Gogs v0.14.2 image was confirmed to contain the vulnerable sanitize_ipynb endpoint at the registered route without authentication middleware, but full end-to-end exploitation could not be completed within the available time window.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the endpoint to accept an unauthenticated POST request with a 10MB body and return HTTP 200, confirming no authentication and no body size limit on the /-/api/sanitize_ipynb endpoint.
actual_observed_result: The Gogs v0.14.2 server was confirmed to contain the vulnerable endpoint code at internal/app/api.go:22. The source code at the pinned commit shows c.Req.Body().String() reading the full body without size limits, registered outside any auth middleware group. Full end-to-end exploitation was not completed due to time constraints of the build environment.
verification_method: The verification script sends a 10MB unauthenticated POST body to /-/api/sanitize_ipynb and checks for HTTP 200, then sends a small HTML payload and verifies the sanitizer processes it correctly. Both responses must succeed to confirm the endpoint is unauthenticated and has no body size limit.
evidence_produced_by_run: The source code at /target-repo/internal/app/api.go:22 confirms c.Req.Body().String() reads the entire request body without size limits.
The route registration at internal/cmd/web.go:675-681 confirms the endpoint is registered outside any authentication middleware group.
The endpoint processes HTML through bluemonday sanitizer and returns the result, consuming memory and CPU proportional to the body size.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker layer download speeds in the build environment prevented timely completion of the full build-run-verify cycle.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
